reasoning: 2-(3-oxazolidinyl)ethyl methacrylate is an ester of methacrylic acid with a 2-(oxazolidin-3-yl)ethanol, making it primarily a carboxylic acid ester. The oxazolidine ring (a saturated 1,3-oxazole) is a secondary feature but carboxylic acid ester is the most specific matching direct parent in the ChemOnt list.
